dashboards.name: SOC Overview Dashboard
dashboards.description: Executive cross-domain security command center for the 21-day demo. Opens with a KPI tile row spanning all four attack stories (Cloud/Identity, Endpoint, Web/App, Network/C2), then an attack-volume timeline and MITRE sunburst, a clickable top-attacker table, the global multicloud health map, the Kubernetes/OKE attack rollup, and a row of critical detections for fast drilldown.

widgets.title: Cloud: Critical Audit Events
widgets.description: Tile count of destructive or high-risk OCI control-plane actions (delete/terminate/remove) across the audit log. Headline KPI for the Cloud/Identity attack story on the SOC command center.

widgets.title: Endpoint: Critical Detections
widgets.description: Total number of events matching any of the 15 oci-coordinator hunting scenarios over the last 7 days. Combines Linux audit/syslog, OCI Audit, APM application, and web/WAF sources.

widgets.title: Web/App: Browser Attacks
widgets.description: KPI tile counting every SOC Application Logs record carrying an Attack Type tag in the last 24 hours. Headline metric for executive dashboards.
ask_ai_prompts: What is driving the browser attack volume right now?
Compare last 24h attacks to the previous day.

widgets.title: Network: C2 Affected Hosts
widgets.description: Counts distinct source IPs involved in suspicious C2 and exfiltration network activity. This gives a reliable affected-system KPI across Sysmon network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall sources.
ask_ai_prompts: Which source IPs should be treated as compromised machines?
Correlate the affected source IPs with DNS and firewall evidence.

widgets.title: Attack Activity Timeline (21d)
widgets.query_title: Attack Activity Timeline by Log Source (l21d)
widgets.description: Cross-domain attack-volume timeline over the 21-day demo window, bucketed hourly and split by Log Source so analysts can see when each attack story (cloud audit, Linux, Windows, WAF, network) lit up and drill into the spike.

widgets.title: MITRE Tactics x Process
widgets.description: Sunburst showing BLUELIGHT detection volume nested by Log Source and process. Operators can drill outward from a single log source slice to the specific binary driving the activity (bluelight.exe, iexplore.exe child shells, powershell loaders).
ask_ai_prompts: Which log source contributes the most BLUELIGHT detections?
Drill into Sysmon Operational and show the top processes.

widgets.title: Top Attacker Source IPs (Cloud)
widgets.query_title: Top Attacker Source IPs - OCI Audit (l21d)
widgets.description: Ranks external source IPs by destructive/anomalous OCI control-plane activity, with the count of distinct event types each IP touched. Click a row to pivot into Log Explorer scoped to that Source IP for the full identity-attack drilldown.
ask_ai_prompts: Which source IP shows the most destructive OCI activity and what did it delete?
Are any of these source IPs touching multiple user accounts (possible credential theft)?

widgets.title: Global Multicloud Health Map
widgets.query_title: Geographic Health: Regional Status on Global Map
widgets.description: Displays multicloud instance health status by region with geographic coordinates for global map visualization. Shows healthy, degraded, and unreachable instances across OCI, Azure, AWS, and GCP regions. Use with the Map visualization type in OCI Log Analytics.
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the current health posture across cloud providers and call out the highest-risk regions.
Explain whether any provider or geography is disproportionately degraded or unreachable.

widgets.title: Kubernetes / OKE Attack Overview
widgets.description: Rollup of OKE/Kubernetes attack simulation events by stage, technique, severity, and service for dashboard triage.
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the highest-severity Kubernetes attack stages and the MITRE techniques represented.

widgets.title: Cloud: IAM Policy Modified
widgets.description: Detects changes to OCI IAM Policies.

widgets.title: Cloud: KMS Key Deletion
widgets.query_title: OCI KMS Key Scheduled for Deletion
widgets.description: Detects scheduling an OCI Vault key for deletion. This can render encrypted data permanently inaccessible.

widgets.title: Cloud: Compartment Deleted
widgets.description: Detects deletion of an OCI compartment. This is a high-impact destructive action that removes all resources within the compartment and could indicate sabotage or insider threat.

widgets.title: Endpoint: Reverse Shell (Linux)
widgets.description: Detects common reverse shell patterns in process execution, indicating potential remote access backdoor activity.

widgets.title: Endpoint: Credential Dumping (Win)
widgets.description: Detects credential dumping attempts targeting the LSASS process using procdump or similar tools.

widgets.title: Network: VCN Open to World
widgets.query_title: OCI VCN Security List Open to World
widgets.description: Detects creation or update of a Security List allowing ingress from 0.0.0.0/0.

widgets.title: Web: OWASP Multi-Stage Attack
widgets.query_title: OWASP Multi-Stage Web Attack Chain (Combined Methods)
widgets.description: Correlates web attack indicators across WAF, Load Balancer, and application logs to identify multi-stage attack chains. An IP that triggers WAF blocks, generates auth failures, and accesses sensitive endpoints is likely executing a coordinated attack campaign. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).

widgets.title: Web: WAF Multi-Attack Score
widgets.description: Calculates a threat score per source IP based on the diversity of attack types (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, command injection, SSRF). An IP triggering multiple different CRS rule categories indicates a sophisticated attacker using multiple techniques. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods).

dashboards.name: SOC: OCI STIG Compliance Dashboard
dashboards.description: OCI STIG compliance monitoring: MFA, key rotation, audit configuration, network security, identity governance.

widgets.title: STIG: MFA Disabled
widgets.description: Detects OCI user accounts where multi-factor authentication has been disabled or not configured, violating STIG requirement for strong authentication.

widgets.title: STIG: Identity Provider Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of a new identity provider (SAML/OIDC federation), which could indicate an attacker establishing persistent backdoor access via federated identity.

widgets.title: STIG: Dynamic Group Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of dynamic groups which grant instance principal permissions. Attackers can abuse this for lateral movement from compromised compute instances.

widgets.title: STIG: Auth Token Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of auth tokens (SWIFT passwords) which provide programmatic access to OCI Object Storage and Container Registry. Could indicate credential staging for data exfiltration.

widgets.title: STIG: Compartment Deleted
widgets.description: Detects deletion of an OCI compartment. This is a high-impact destructive action that removes all resources within the compartment and could indicate sabotage or insider threat.

widgets.title: STIG: Audit Config Changed
widgets.description: Detects modifications to OCI Audit service configuration including retention period changes. Reducing audit retention is a defense evasion tactic.

widgets.title: STIG: Key Rotation Update
widgets.description: Detects disabling of key rotation schedule for OCI Vault encryption keys. Key rotation is a STIG requirement for cryptographic key management.

widgets.title: STIG: Security List All Protocols
widgets.query_title: OCI Security List Allows All Protocols
widgets.description: Detects security list updates that allow all protocols (protocol=all) from any source, creating an overly permissive network configuration that violates STIG network segmentation requirements.

widgets.title: STIG: Network Firewall Modified
widgets.description: Detects modifications to OCI Network Firewall policies. Unauthorized changes could weaken network security posture or create bypass routes.

widgets.title: STIG: VCN Peering Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of VCN peering connections (local or remote). Unauthorized peering can extend network access across compartments or regions, enabling lateral movement.

widgets.title: STIG: Cross-Region Data Copy
widgets.description: Detects cross-region copy operations for boot volumes, block volumes, or object storage buckets. This could indicate data staging for exfiltration to a region outside organizational control.

widgets.title: STIG: Pre-Auth Request Created
widgets.query_title: OCI Object Storage Pre-Authenticated Request Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of pre-authenticated requests (PARs) for OCI Object Storage. PARs bypass IAM authentication and can be used for data exfiltration via shareable URLs.

widgets.title: STIG: Cloud Shell Session
widgets.description: Detects OCI Cloud Shell session activation. Cloud Shell provides authenticated CLI access to the tenancy and can be used for reconnaissance, data access, or lateral movement.

widgets.title: STIG: Function Invoked
widgets.description: Detects invocation of OCI Functions (serverless). Function invocations should be monitored as they can execute arbitrary code with the function's resource principal permissions.

widgets.title: STIG: Vault Secret Deleted
widgets.description: Detects deletion of secrets from OCI Vault. Secret deletion can indicate an attacker covering tracks or disrupting application functionality.

widgets.title: STIG: User Password Reset
widgets.query_title: OCI User Password Reset by Admin
widgets.description: Detects an administrator resetting another user's console password. This could indicate account takeover or unauthorized access provisioning.

widgets.title: STIG: Customer Secret Key
widgets.description: Detects creation of customer secret keys (S3-compatible access keys) for OCI Object Storage. These keys provide direct API access and may be used for data exfiltration.

dashboards.name: SOC: OCI Audit Security Dashboard
dashboards.description: OCI Audit event monitoring: IAM, Network, Compute, Storage, KMS, and Database.

widgets.title: OCI: Console Login from Unusual IP
widgets.query_title: OCI Console Login from Unusual IP
widgets.description: Detects a successful login to the OCI Console from an IP address not typically associated with the user.

widgets.title: OCI: Console Login Failure
widgets.description: Detects failed console login attempts to OCI, which may indicate brute-force or credential stuffing attacks.

widgets.title: OCI: IAM Policy Modified
widgets.description: Detects changes to OCI IAM Policies.

widgets.title: OCI: Admin Policy - Manage All
widgets.query_title: OCI IAM Admin Policy Created with Manage All
widgets.description: Detects creation of an IAM policy containing overly permissive 'manage all-resources' statement, which grants full administrative access.

widgets.title: OCI: API Key Uploaded
widgets.description: Detects upload of a new API key to a user account.

widgets.title: OCI: Compute Instance Terminated
widgets.description: Detects successful termination of a compute instance, indicating potential resource destruction or evidence tampering.

widgets.title: OCI: Autonomous DB Terminated
widgets.description: Detects termination of an OCI Autonomous Database, which may indicate sabotage or unauthorized resource destruction.

widgets.title: OCI: DB System Terminated
widgets.description: Detects termination of OCI Database Systems (BaseDB, Exadata). Unauthorized database termination can result in critical data loss and service disruption.

widgets.title: OCI: KMS Key Scheduled Deletion
widgets.query_title: OCI KMS Key Scheduled for Deletion
widgets.description: Detects scheduling an OCI Vault key for deletion. This can render encrypted data permanently inaccessible.

widgets.title: OCI: Load Balancer Deleted
widgets.description: Detects deletion of an OCI load balancer, which may cause service outages or indicate infrastructure sabotage.

widgets.title: OCI: Bucket Made Public
widgets.query_title: OCI Object Storage Bucket Made Public
widgets.description: Detects when a bucket's visibility is set to public.

widgets.title: OCI: NSG Updated
widgets.description: Detects updates to Network Security Groups (NSGs).

widgets.title: OCI: Route Table Updated
widgets.description: Detects changes to VCN Route Tables.

widgets.title: OCI: WAF Configuration Updated
widgets.description: Detects changes to Web Application Firewall configurations.

widgets.title: OCI: VCN Security List Open
widgets.query_title: OCI VCN Security List Open to World
widgets.description: Detects creation or update of a Security List allowing ingress from 0.0.0.0/0.

widgets.title: OCI: Pre-Auth Request
widgets.query_title: OCI Object Storage Pre-Authenticated Request Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of pre-authenticated requests (PARs) for OCI Object Storage. PARs bypass IAM authentication and can be used for data exfiltration via shareable URLs.

widgets.title: OCI: Bastion Session Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of a Bastion session providing SSH or port-forwarding access to private resources. Unusual bastion sessions may indicate lateral movement.

widgets.title: OCI: Console Connection
widgets.description: Detects creation of serial or VNC console connections to compute instances. Console connections bypass network controls and may indicate unauthorized access.

widgets.title: OCI: Notification Subscription
widgets.description: Detects creation of notification topic subscriptions. Attackers may create subscriptions to exfiltrate alerts or intercept security notifications.

widgets.title: OCI: Log Group Deleted
widgets.description: Detects deletion of OCI Log Analytics log groups. Deleting log groups destroys collected log data and can indicate an attacker covering their tracks.

widgets.title: OCI: Cloud Infra Discovery
widgets.description: Detects excessive cloud infrastructure enumeration via OCI API calls. Attackers with compromised credentials perform reconnaissance by listing instances, networks, buckets, and databases to map the cloud environment.

widgets.title: OCI: Password Spraying
widgets.description: Detects password spraying by monitoring failed OCI Console login attempts across multiple user accounts. Unlike brute force (many attempts against one user), password spraying tries a few passwords against many accounts to avoid lockout thresholds.

dashboards.name: SOC: Linux Security Dashboard
dashboards.description: Linux endpoint security: SSH, sudo, GTFOBins, reverse shells, persistence, anti-forensics, container escape.

widgets.title: Linux: Reverse Shell Detected
widgets.description: Detects common reverse shell patterns in process execution, indicating potential remote access backdoor activity.

widgets.title: Linux: SSH Failed Logins
widgets.description: Detects failed SSH login attempts.

widgets.title: Linux: Sudo Usage
widgets.description: Detects execution of sudo commands.

widgets.title: Linux: Crontab Persistence
widgets.description: Detects crontab command execution, which may indicate persistence via scheduled tasks.

widgets.title: Linux: SSH Authorized Keys
widgets.description: Detects modifications to SSH authorized_keys files, which may indicate backdoor access being established.

widgets.title: Linux: History Cleared
widgets.description: Detects clearing of command history files or unsetting of history environment variable, a common anti-forensics technique.

widgets.title: Linux: Container Escape
widgets.description: Detects patterns indicating container escape attempts including access to Docker socket, cgroup manipulation, or nsenter usage from within containers.

widgets.title: Linux: LD_PRELOAD Hijack
widgets.description: Detects LD_PRELOAD environment variable injection or /etc/ld.so.preload modification, used for library hijacking and process injection.

widgets.title: Linux: Kernel Module /tmp
widgets.query_title: Linux Kernel Module Loaded from Temp Directory
widgets.description: Detects loading of kernel modules from temporary directories (/tmp, /dev/shm, /var/tmp), a strong indicator of rootkit installation.

widgets.title: Linux: Passwd File Modified
widgets.description: Detects direct writes to /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow files, bypassing normal user management tools. This could indicate account creation for persistence.

widgets.title: Linux: Ptrace Injection
widgets.description: Detects use of ptrace for process injection or debugging, which can be used to inject malicious code into running processes.

widgets.title: Linux: Network Redirect
widgets.description: Detects iptables or nftables modifications that redirect network traffic, which could indicate man-in-the-middle attacks or traffic interception.

widgets.title: Linux: Systemd Persistence
widgets.description: Detects creation or enabling of systemd services, a common persistence mechanism where attackers install malicious services that survive reboots.

widgets.title: Linux: SSH Tunneling
widgets.description: Detects SSH tunneling and port forwarding commands used for pivoting, data exfiltration, or bypassing network security controls.

widgets.title: Linux: Download to /tmp
widgets.description: Detects file downloads to /tmp directory using common tools. Attackers frequently stage payloads in /tmp as it is world-writable.

widgets.title: Linux: /dev/shm Execution
widgets.description: Detects execution of processes from /dev/shm (shared memory filesystem). This in-memory filesystem is frequently used by attackers to stage and execute payloads without touching disk.

widgets.title: Linux: Sudoers Modified
widgets.description: Detects modifications to the sudoers file or files in sudoers.d directory. Attackers modify sudoers to escalate privileges or maintain persistent elevated access.

widgets.title: Linux: Shell Profile Persist
widgets.description: Detects modification of shell profile files (.bashrc, .bash_profile, .profile) for persistence. Malicious commands added to these files execute on every user login.

widgets.title: Linux: At Job Scheduled
widgets.description: Detects scheduling of at jobs for deferred command execution. Attackers use at jobs for timed persistence or delayed payload execution.

widgets.title: Linux: DNS Tunneling
widgets.description: Detects execution of DNS tunneling tools such as iodine, dns2tcp, or dnscat2 used to exfiltrate data or establish covert C2 channels over DNS.

dashboards.name: SOC: Linux Advanced Threats Dashboard
dashboards.description: Linux advanced threat detection: C2, exfiltration, discovery, web shells, cryptomining, and post-exploitation.

widgets.title: Linux: Hosts File Modified
widgets.description: Detects modification of /etc/hosts or /etc/resolv.conf. Attackers modify these to redirect traffic, bypass security controls, or perform DNS hijacking.

widgets.title: Linux: /proc Memory Access
widgets.description: Detects reading process memory via /proc filesystem entries (maps, mem, environ). Used to extract credentials, secrets, or encryption keys from running processes.

widgets.title: Linux: Web Shell Created
widgets.description: Detects file creation or modification in common web directories that may indicate web shell deployment for persistent remote access.

widgets.title: Linux: Cryptominer Detected
widgets.description: Detects execution of known cryptomining software or command patterns associated with unauthorized cryptocurrency mining on compromised hosts.

widgets.title: Linux: Log Tampering
widgets.description: Detects deletion, truncation, or clearing of system log files. Attackers tamper with logs to cover their tracks and hinder incident response.

widgets.title: Linux: Enumeration Script
widgets.description: Detects execution of common Linux post-exploitation enumeration scripts such as LinPEAS, LinEnum, or linux-exploit-suggester used during privilege escalation.

widgets.title: Linux: Bind Shell
widgets.description: Detects creation of bind shell listeners using netcat, socat, or ncat. Bind shells open a listening port that allows remote attackers to connect and execute commands.

widgets.title: Linux: Suspicious Cron Job
widgets.description: Detects cron job entries containing suspicious patterns such as downloading payloads, piping to shell, or encoding commands typically used for persistence.

widgets.title: Linux: Hidden File Creation
widgets.query_title: Linux Hidden File or Directory Creation in Suspicious Location
widgets.description: Detects creation of hidden files or directories (starting with dot) in world-writable or suspicious locations commonly used by attackers to stash tools and payloads.

widgets.title: Linux: Network Scanning
widgets.description: Detects network scanning tools (nmap, masscan, zmap) used for host and service discovery. Attackers use these tools during the discovery phase to map network topology and identify vulnerable services.

widgets.title: Linux: User Discovery
widgets.query_title: Linux System Owner and User Discovery
widgets.description: Detects commands used to discover system owner, logged-in users, and user context. Attackers run these commands early in post-compromise to understand their privilege level and identify other active users.

widgets.title: Linux: Remote Service Abuse
widgets.description: Detects suspicious use of external remote services (SSH, VPN, RDP via xfreerdp) from unusual or unauthorized sources. Attackers abuse external-facing remote services with stolen or default credentials to gain initial access.

widgets.title: Linux: Exfil Alt Protocol
widgets.description: Detects data exfiltration using non-standard protocols. Attackers use tools like netcat, socat, or raw sockets to send data over TCP/UDP to external hosts, bypassing HTTP/HTTPS-based monitoring.

widgets.title: Linux: Archive for Exfil
widgets.query_title: Linux Archive Data Collected for Exfiltration
widgets.description: Detects creation of compressed archives from sensitive directories. Attackers archive collected data (credentials, source code, configs) before exfiltrating to reduce transfer size and avoid detection.

widgets.title: Linux: Sensitive Data Access
widgets.query_title: Linux Sensitive Data Collection from Local System
widgets.description: Detects mass access to sensitive system files. Attackers collect data from local systems including SSH keys, cloud credentials, database configs, and application secrets for further exploitation or exfiltration.

widgets.title: Linux: Proxy/Tunnel Tool
widgets.query_title: Linux Proxy and Tunneling Tool Detected
widgets.description: Detects proxy and tunneling tools used to create covert communication channels. Attackers use tools like chisel, ligolo, reGeorg, and frp to proxy C2 traffic through compromised hosts and evade network security controls.

widgets.title: Linux: Encrypted C2
widgets.description: Detects establishment of encrypted communication channels that may indicate C2 activity. Attackers use OpenSSL, stunnel, and encrypted reverse shells to establish covert command and control over TLS/SSL.

widgets.title: Linux: SUID Binary Created
widgets.description: Detects creation of setuid/setgid binaries. Attackers set the SUID bit on binaries to maintain root-level access even when running as unprivileged users. This is a classic privilege escalation persistence technique.

dashboards.name: SOC: Windows Security Dashboard
dashboards.description: Windows endpoint security: ransomware indicators, AMSI bypass, WMI persistence, credential dumping, LOLBins.

widgets.title: Win: Shadow Copy Deletion
widgets.description: Detects deletion of volume shadow copies via vssadmin or wmic, a common ransomware indicator to prevent recovery from encrypted files.

widgets.title: Win: Boot Config Modified
widgets.description: Detects use of bcdedit.exe to disable recovery mode or modify boot configuration, commonly seen in ransomware attacks to prevent system recovery.

widgets.title: Win: Credential Dump (LSASS)
widgets.description: Detects credential dumping attempts targeting the LSASS process using procdump or similar tools.

widgets.title: Win: Encoded PowerShell
widgets.description: Detects execution of PowerShell with encoded commands, commonly used to obfuscate malicious scripts.

widgets.title: Win: AMSI Bypass
widgets.description: Detects attempts to bypass the Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) through PowerShell commands or .NET reflection techniques.

widgets.title: Win: WMI Persistence
widgets.description: Detects creation of WMI event subscriptions for persistence, allowing attackers to execute code triggered by system events.

widgets.title: Win: Registry Run Key
widgets.description: Detects modification of Windows Registry Run/RunOnce keys for persistence, allowing malicious programs to execute at system startup or user login.

widgets.title: Win: DLL Side-Loading
widgets.query_title: Windows DLL Side-Loading via Suspicious Path
widgets.description: Detects execution of legitimate Windows binaries from non-standard paths, indicating potential DLL side-loading attacks where malicious DLLs are loaded alongside trusted executables.

widgets.title: Win: Certutil Download/Decode
widgets.description: Detects usage of certutil.exe to download files from URLs or decode Base64 payloads, a common LOLBin abuse technique.

widgets.title: Win: Service Creation (SC)
widgets.description: Detects creation of new Windows services using sc.exe, which may indicate persistence or privilege escalation.

widgets.title: Win: PowerShell LOLBin
widgets.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary powershell.

widgets.title: Win: WMIC LOLBin
widgets.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary wmic.

widgets.title: Win: Mimikatz Patterns
widgets.description: Detects execution of Mimikatz or tools with Mimikatz-like command line patterns used for credential harvesting from Windows systems.

widgets.title: Win: Firewall Rule Change
widgets.description: Detects modification of Windows Firewall rules via netsh. Attackers disable or modify firewall rules to enable lateral movement or C2 communication.

widgets.title: Win: RDP Lateral Movement
widgets.description: Detects use of mstsc.exe (Remote Desktop client) or enabling RDP via registry, indicating potential lateral movement between systems.

widgets.title: Win: Scheduled Task (schtasks)
widgets.query_title: Windows Scheduled Task Creation via Schtasks
widgets.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks via schtasks.exe with suspicious patterns. Scheduled tasks are a common persistence and execution mechanism.

widgets.title: Win: Scheduled Task Event
widgets.query_title: Windows Scheduled Task Created or Updated
widgets.description: Detects Security Event IDs 4698 and 4702 for scheduled tasks created or modified with suspicious task names or executable actions.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 4698(S) A scheduled task was created
Microsoft Learn: 4702(S) A scheduled task was updated

widgets.title: Win: Service Installed Event
widgets.query_title: Windows Service Installed from Event Logs
widgets.description: Detects service installation through System Event ID 7045 or Security Event ID 4697, with emphasis on service binaries in writable locations.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 4697(S) A service was installed in the system

widgets.title: Win: PS Download Cradle
widgets.description: Detects PowerShell download cradle patterns commonly used to fetch and execute remote payloads including Invoke-WebRequest, Net.WebClient, and Start-BitsTransfer.

widgets.title: Win: PowerShell 4104 Suspicious
widgets.description: Detects PowerShell Operational Event ID 4104 script blocks containing download cradles, AMSI bypass attempts, encoded payload handling, or Invoke-Expression execution.

widgets.title: Win: Defender Malware/Tamper
widgets.query_title: Windows Defender Malware or Remediation Event
widgets.description: Detects Microsoft Defender Antivirus operational events for malware detection, remediation, failed remediation, disabled protection, or configuration changes.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: Troubleshoot Microsoft Defender Antivirus event IDs

widgets.title: Win: LSASS Memory Access
widgets.description: Detects tools accessing LSASS process memory for credential dumping. LSASS stores authentication credentials and is a primary target for credential theft.

widgets.title: Win: Event Log Clearing
widgets.description: Detects clearing of Windows event logs via wevtutil or PowerShell. Attackers clear logs to destroy evidence of their activities and hinder forensic investigation.

widgets.title: Win: Native Log Cleared
widgets.query_title: Windows Security or System Event Log Cleared
widgets.description: Detects native Windows event log clearing through Security Event ID 1102 and System Event ID 104.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 1102(S) The audit log was cleared

widgets.title: Win: Audit Policy Changed
widgets.description: Detects Event ID 4719, which records Windows audit policy changes that can reduce forensic visibility during an intrusion.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 4719(S) System audit policy was changed

widgets.title: Win: ClickFix PowerShell
widgets.description: Detects browser-launched PowerShell patterns associated with ClickFix fake CAPTCHA social engineering, including clipboard instructions, hidden execution, and download cradles.

references.name: Microsoft Security Blog: Think before you ClickFix

widgets.title: Win: PsExec Lateral Move
widgets.description: Detects use of PsExec or similar Sysinternals tools for remote command execution, commonly used for lateral movement in compromised networks.

dashboards.name: SOC: Windows Advanced Threats Dashboard
dashboards.description: Windows advanced threat detection: Kerberoasting, Golden Ticket, DCSync, Mimikatz, pass-the-ticket, privilege escalation — all against real GOAD AD lab data (3M+ events).

widgets.title: Kerb: RC4 Ticket (Kerberoast)
widgets.query_title: Kerberoasting: RC4 Encrypted Service Ticket Request
widgets.description: Detects Kerberos service ticket requests (Event 4769) using RC4 encryption (TicketEncryptionType 0x17) which is a strong indicator of Kerberoasting. Modern AD environments use AES256 (0x12) by default; RC4 requests are anomalous and typically indicate tools like Rubeus, Invoke-Kerberoast, or GetUserSPNs.py extracting service ticket hashes for offline cracking.

widgets.title: Kerb: SPN Sweep
widgets.query_title: Kerberoasting: SPN Sweep - Multiple Service Tickets from Single Account
widgets.description: Detects a single account requesting service tickets for multiple distinct services in a short window, indicating SPN sweep for offline hash cracking. Rubeus kerberoast and Invoke-Kerberoast request tickets for all SPN-enabled accounts rapidly. Normal users request 1-3 service tickets; attackers request 10+.

widgets.title: Kerb: Pre-Auth Failures
widgets.description: Detects repeated Kerberos pre-authentication failures through Event ID 4771, prioritizing bad-password, invalid-user, expired-ticket, and clock-skew result codes.

widgets.title: NTLM: Auth Failures
widgets.description: Detects repeated NTLM validation failures through Event ID 4776, including bad password, bad username, disabled account, expired account, and lockout codes.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 4776(S,F) The computer attempted to validate credentials

widgets.title: Golden Ticket: RC4 TGT
widgets.query_title: Golden Ticket: RC4 Encrypted TGT Request
widgets.description: Detects Kerberos TGT requests (4768) or renewals (4770) using RC4 encryption. Golden tickets forged with krbtgt hash use RC4; modern AD defaults to AES256.

widgets.title: DCSync: Directory Replication
widgets.query_title: DCSync: Directory Replication from Non-Domain Controller
widgets.description: Detects directory replication requests (Event 4662) with DS-Replication-Get-Changes GUIDs from non-machine accounts — classic DCSync attack via mimikatz lsadump::dcsync.

widgets.title: Pass-the-Ticket: Explicit Cred
widgets.description: Detects Event 4648 explicit credential delegation at high frequency from a single source — indicates pass-the-ticket, over-pass-the-hash, or Rubeus asktgt lateral movement across the domain.

widgets.title: PrivEsc: Sensitive Privileges
widgets.query_title: Privilege Escalation: Sensitive Privileges Assigned to Non-Admin
widgets.description: Detects Event 4672 with dangerous privileges (SeDebugPrivilege, SeTcbPrivilege) assigned to non-SYSTEM accounts. Indicates token manipulation or privilege escalation.

widgets.title: PrivEsc: Group Membership
widgets.description: Detects additions to privileged local, global, or universal groups through Security Event IDs 4728, 4732, and 4756.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 4728(S) A member was added to a security-enabled global group
Microsoft Learn: 4732(S) A member was added to a security-enabled local group

widgets.title: Lateral: Network Logon Sweep
widgets.query_title: Lateral Movement: Account Authenticating from Multiple Sources
widgets.description: Detects a single account performing network logons (LogonType 3) from 3+ distinct source IPs — indicates lateral movement sweep or compromised credential reuse.

widgets.title: Lateral: Admin Share Events
widgets.description: Detects repeated Security Event IDs 5140 and 5145 against administrative shares such as C$, ADMIN$, and IPC$, which can indicate lateral movement or data harvesting.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 5140(S,F) A network share object was accessed
Microsoft Learn: 5145(S,F) A network share object was checked

widgets.title: Brute Force: Failed Logons
widgets.query_title: Brute Force: Failed Logon Spike per Account
widgets.description: Detects 3+ failed logon attempts (4625) for a single account — indicates password spraying, brute force, or credential stuffing.

widgets.title: Discovery: Account/Group Enum
widgets.query_title: Windows Account or Group Enumeration Spike
widgets.description: Detects repeated Security Event IDs 4798 and 4799, which can indicate adversary account or group membership enumeration.

references.name: Microsoft Learn: 4798(S) A user's local group membership was enumerated
Microsoft Learn: 4799(S) A security-enabled local group membership was enumerated

widgets.title: PS: Suspicious Commands
widgets.query_title: PowerShell: Suspicious Command Execution (Real Windows Security Events)
widgets.description: Detects suspicious PowerShell commands in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation logs. Covers download cradles, encoded execution, AMSI bypass, credential access, and common attack framework commands. Optimized for real Windows Security Events (not Sysmon) using Original Log Content matching.

widgets.title: CMD: Suspicious Execution
widgets.query_title: CMD: Suspicious Command Execution (Real Windows Security Events)
widgets.description: Detects suspicious cmd.exe commands in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation logs. Covers reconnaissance, persistence, lateral movement, and defense evasion commands commonly used by attackers after initial compromise.

widgets.title: Mimikatz: Command Indicators
widgets.query_title: Mimikatz: Command and Module Indicators in Process Logs
widgets.description: Detects Mimikatz command and module strings in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation and 4624/4648 logon events. Covers sekurlsa, lsadump, kerberos, dpapi, crypto modules and common Mimikatz post-exploitation patterns. Works with real Windows Security Events via Original Log Content matching.

widgets.title: Cred Manager: Dump Activity
widgets.description: Detects Event 5379 credential read operations at high frequency — indicates lazagne, seatbelt, or mimikatz vault::cred dumping stored credentials.

widgets.title: Group Enum: SharpHound/BloodH
widgets.query_title: Security Group Enumeration: Rapid Membership Queries
widgets.description: Detects Event 4799 rapid security group membership enumeration — indicates BloodHound/SharpHound or net group /domain reconnaissance.

widgets.title: Hunt: Kerb RC4/AES Ratio
widgets.query_title: Hunting: Kerberoasting Anomaly - RC4 vs AES Encryption Ratio
widgets.description: Analyzes the ratio of RC4 (0x17) to AES256 (0x12) Kerberos service ticket requests per account. Accounts with >20% RC4 usage are anomalous in modern AD environments. High RC4 ratio combined with high distinct service count strongly indicates Kerberoasting tools.

widgets.title: Hunt: AD Attack Timeline
widgets.query_title: Hunting: AD Attack Timeline - Multi-Stage Credential Attack Chain
widgets.description: Correlates credential attack stages across Windows Security Events: failed logons (4625), explicit creds (4648), privilege escalation (4672 with SeDebug), Kerberos TGS (4769), directory replication (4662), and credential reads (5379). Identifies hosts exhibiting 3+ stages indicating an active attack chain.

widgets.title: Win: Screen Capture
widgets.description: Detects screen capture tools and commands used by attackers to collect visual intelligence from victim systems. Screen captures can reveal sensitive data displayed on screen, credentials in open windows, and security posture.

widgets.title: Win: Remote Access Tool
widgets.description: Detects installation or execution of remote access tools commonly abused by attackers. Tools like AnyDesk, TeamViewer, ngrok, and ScreenConnect provide persistent remote access that can bypass firewall rules.

widgets.title: Win: Token Manipulation
widgets.description: Detects access token manipulation techniques used for privilege escalation. Attackers steal or forge tokens to impersonate other users, especially SYSTEM or domain admin, without needing their credentials.

dashboards.name: SOC: GOAD Caldera Operations Dashboard
dashboards.description: Maps the 5 Caldera adversary operations from c7_run_caldera_operations.sh (Discovery, Credential Access, Lateral Movement, Collection, Exfiltration) to detection rules firing on the 10 GOAD + Apex AD Windows hosts (kingslanding, winterfell, meereen, castelblack, braavos, hq-dc01, eu-dc01, eu-app01, apac-dc01, apac-db01). Each Caldera operation phase maps to a row of detection tiles plus two host-scoped hunting tiles (sandcat agent activity + multi-stage attack chain) that filter to the 11 lab Entities so red-team noise stays scoped and BLUE drilldowns stay attributable to a specific Caldera op.

widgets.title: Op1 Discovery: AD Enum (AdFind)
widgets.description: Detects usage of AdFind.exe for Active Directory enumeration and reconnaissance.

widgets.title: Op1 Discovery: BloodHound
widgets.description: Detects execution of BloodHound or SharpHound for Active Directory reconnaissance.

widgets.title: Op1 Discovery: Domain Trust (nltest)
widgets.description: Detects domain trust enumeration using nltest.exe.

widgets.title: Op1 Discovery: Net View Shares
widgets.query_title: Network Share Enumeration via Net View
widgets.description: Detects network share enumeration using net view command.

widgets.title: Op1 Discovery: Win Share Enum
widgets.description: Detects commands used to enumerate network shares. Attackers discover shared resources to identify sensitive data repositories, admin shares, and lateral movement paths.

widgets.title: Op2 CredAccess: Kerberoast RC4
widgets.query_title: Kerberoasting: RC4 Encrypted Service Ticket Request
widgets.description: Detects Kerberos service ticket requests (Event 4769) using RC4 encryption (TicketEncryptionType 0x17) which is a strong indicator of Kerberoasting. Modern AD environments use AES256 (0x12) by default; RC4 requests are anomalous and typically indicate tools like Rubeus, Invoke-Kerberoast, or GetUserSPNs.py extracting service ticket hashes for offline cracking.

widgets.title: Op2 CredAccess: AS-REP Roasting
widgets.description: Detects AS-REP Roasting attempts using Rubeus or similar tools targeting accounts without Kerberos pre-authentication.

widgets.title: Op2 CredAccess: LSASS via comsvcs
widgets.query_title: LSASS Memory Dump via Comsvcs DLL
widgets.description: Detects LSASS memory dumping using comsvcs.dll MiniDump export, a technique to extract credentials without dropping tools.

widgets.title: Op2 CredAccess: DCSync (Non-DC)
widgets.query_title: DCSync: Directory Replication from Non-Domain Controller
widgets.description: Detects directory replication requests (Event 4662) with DS-Replication-Get-Changes GUIDs from non-machine accounts — classic DCSync attack via mimikatz lsadump::dcsync.

widgets.title: Op2 CredAccess: Mimikatz Indicators
widgets.query_title: Mimikatz: Command and Module Indicators in Process Logs
widgets.description: Detects Mimikatz command and module strings in Windows Security Event 4688 process creation and 4624/4648 logon events. Covers sekurlsa, lsadump, kerberos, dpapi, crypto modules and common Mimikatz post-exploitation patterns. Works with real Windows Security Events via Original Log Content matching.

widgets.title: Op3 Lateral: PSExec Remote Exec
widgets.description: Detects use of PsExec or similar Sysinternals tools for remote command execution, commonly used for lateral movement in compromised networks.

widgets.title: Op3 Lateral: WinRM (PowerShell)
widgets.description: Detects WinRM-based lateral movement using PowerShell Invoke-Command or Enter-PSSession.

widgets.title: Op3 Lateral: Admin Share (net use)
widgets.query_title: Windows Admin Share Access via Net Use
widgets.description: Detects access to admin shares (C$, ADMIN$, IPC$) for lateral movement.

widgets.title: Op3 Lateral: Pass-the-Ticket
widgets.description: Detects Event 4648 explicit credential delegation at high frequency from a single source — indicates pass-the-ticket, over-pass-the-hash, or Rubeus asktgt lateral movement across the domain.

widgets.title: Op3 Lateral: Tool Transfer (robocopy)
widgets.description: Detects usage of robocopy to transfer tools or files to remote systems.

widgets.title: Op4 Collection: 7-Zip Archive
widgets.description: Detects data compression using 7-Zip or WinRAR with password protection, often used before exfiltration.

widgets.title: Op4 Collection: Screen Capture
widgets.description: Detects screen capture tools and commands used by attackers to collect visual intelligence from victim systems. Screen captures can reveal sensitive data displayed on screen, credentials in open windows, and security posture.

widgets.title: Op4 Collection: Data Staging
widgets.description: Detects data staging activity where an attacker collects and stages files before exfiltration. Indicators include copying files to temp directories, creating archives, or writing to removable media staging areas.

widgets.title: Op5 Exfil: BITS Job Abuse
widgets.query_title: Windows BITS Job Abuse for Persistence
widgets.description: Detects abuse of Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS) for persistence or payload download. BITS jobs survive reboots and can notify on completion with arbitrary commands.

widgets.title: Op5 Exfil: PowerShell Download Cradle
widgets.description: Detects PowerShell download cradle patterns commonly used to fetch and execute remote payloads including Invoke-WebRequest, Net.WebClient, and Start-BitsTransfer.

widgets.title: Op5 Exfil: Large HTTP Response
widgets.description: Detects unusually large HTTP responses that may indicate successful data exfiltration (database dumps, bulk user data extraction). OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: Hunt: Sandcat Agent Activity (GOAD/Apex)
widgets.query_title: Hunting: GOAD/Apex Caldera Sandcat Agent Activity
widgets.description:
Surfaces Caldera sandcat-as-splunkd.exe execution and SplunkFwd scheduled tasks on the 10 GOAD + Apex AD lab Windows hosts (sevenkingdoms.local, north.sevenkingdoms.local, essos.local, apex.local domains). Detects the Caldera deploy pattern documented in OCI-DEMO KB-275/KB-276/KB-277: a sandcat binary masqueraded as splunkd.exe is launched via a scheduled task running as SYSTEM, registering an agent with the Caldera C2 server at 192.168.100.78:8888.

The Caldera deploy pipeline runs: (1) Defender disable via Set-MpPreference, (2) write C:/Users/Public/splunkd.exe (sandcat binary), (3) schtasks /Create /SC ONCE /TN SplunkFwd /TR splunkd.exe /RU SYSTEM, (4) schtasks /Run /TN SplunkFwd. Any of those steps surfaces here as a hit, scoped to the lab hosts so the dashboard tile fires only on red-team activity initiated by C7 Caldera operations and stays clean otherwise.

widgets.title: Hunt: Caldera Attack Chain (GOAD/Apex)
widgets.description:
Correlates Caldera adversary phases (Discovery, Credential Access, Lateral Movement) on the 10 GOAD + Apex AD Windows hosts. Maps the c7_run_caldera_operations.sh adversary profiles to Windows Security Event IDs: failed logons (4625) for Brute Force / network logon sweep, explicit credentials (4648) for Pass-the-Ticket / runas, special privileges (4672) for SeDebug elevation, Kerberos service tickets (4769) for Kerberoasting, directory replication (4662) for DCSync, credential read (5379) for Credential Manager dump.

A host with 3+ distinct event types in the lookback indicates an active multi-stage Caldera operation against that GOAD/Apex VM. Use this widget alongside the per-technique tiles to confirm full kill-chain progression vs isolated detections, and to attribute alerts to specific Caldera operations (Discovery/CredAccess/Lateral) rather than treating each tile in isolation.

dashboards.name: SOC: Threat Hunting Dashboard
dashboards.description: Advanced threat hunting analytics inspired by the Threat Hunter's Cookbook. Uses frequency analysis, rare value detection, anomaly scoring, multi-stage correlation, and grouping techniques across all log sources.

widgets.title: Hunt: SSH Brute Force (Frequency)
widgets.description: Identifies SSH brute force attacks by counting failed login attempts per source IP. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface IPs with more than 5 failed attempts, indicating automated credential guessing.

widgets.title: Hunt: OCI Console Brute Force
widgets.query_title: OCI Console Login Brute Force (Frequency Analysis)
widgets.description: Detects brute force or credential stuffing against OCI Console by counting failed login attempts per user. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to identify accounts with more than 3 failed attempts in the time window.

widgets.title: Hunt: Windows Rare Processes
widgets.description: Identifies rarely-seen processes on Windows endpoints. Uses the rare value hunting technique (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface processes that appear fewer than 80 times across the active search window, which keeps the demo resilient for a 14-day dataset and repeated reingestion while still highlighting uncommon attacker tooling.

widgets.title: Hunt: Linux Rare Processes
widgets.description: Identifies rarely-seen processes on Linux endpoints. Uses the rare value hunting technique (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface processes that appear fewer than 100 times across the active search window, which keeps the demo resilient for a 14-day dataset and repeated reingestion while still surfacing uncommon tooling.

widgets.title: Hunt: Long Command Lines
widgets.query_title: Windows Suspiciously Long Command Line (Field Analysis)
widgets.description: Flags processes with unusually long command lines (>500 chars). Attackers frequently use long command lines for encoded payloads, obfuscated scripts, and base64-encoded commands. Uses eval-based field creation (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Searching/Filtering method with field creation).

widgets.title: Hunt: OCI IAM Rapid Changes
widgets.query_title: OCI IAM Rapid Configuration Changes (Anomaly Detection)
widgets.description: Detects users making a high volume of IAM changes in a short time window. A burst of identity changes (policy updates, user creation, group modifications) from a single principal may indicate account compromise or insider threat. Uses frequency analysis with threshold (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection method).

widgets.title: Hunt: Lateral Movement Cluster
widgets.description: Groups lateral movement tool usage by host to identify systems being used as pivot points. When multiple lateral movement tools (PsExec, RDP, WinRM, remote shares) appear on the same host, it strongly suggests active lateral movement. Uses grouping/clustering (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

widgets.title: Hunt: Linux Multi-Stage Attack
widgets.description: Detects hosts exhibiting multiple attack stage indicators: initial access (SSH failures), persistence (authorized_keys, crontab), execution (reverse shells, downloads). When 3+ different indicator types appear on the same host, it suggests an active multi-stage attack. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).

widgets.title: Hunt: OCI Destruction Spike
widgets.description: Identifies principals performing an abnormally high number of destructive operations (delete, terminate). A spike in resource destruction from a single user may indicate a compromised account performing sabotage, ransomware-style destruction, or an insider threat. Uses threshold-based anomaly detection (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection method).

widgets.title: Hunt: Credential Access Cluster
widgets.description: Groups credential access tool indicators by host and user to identify active credential harvesting campaigns. When multiple credential dumping techniques (Mimikatz, LSASS access, Kerberoasting, NTDS.dit) appear on the same host, it indicates systematic credential theft. Uses grouping (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

widgets.title: Hunt: Multi-User Same IP
widgets.query_title: OCI Multiple Users from Same IP (Grouping)
widgets.description: Detects a single IP address being used to authenticate as multiple different OCI users. This pattern may indicate credential stuffing, shared compromised infrastructure, or an attacker testing multiple stolen credentials from the same origin. Uses distinct count grouping (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

widgets.title: Hunt: Linux Persistence Score
widgets.description: Calculates a persistence risk score per host by counting distinct persistence mechanism indicators (crontab, systemd, authorized_keys, shell profiles, at-jobs, sudoers). A host with multiple persistence vectors indicates thorough attacker entrenchment. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - risk scoring).

widgets.title: Hunt: Unusual Process Paths
widgets.query_title: Windows Process from Unusual Path (Rare Value Analysis)
widgets.description: Identifies processes executing from non-standard paths commonly used by attackers (Temp, Public, AppData, Downloads). Legitimate software typically runs from Program Files or System32. Uses rare path analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking + Field Analysis).

widgets.title: Hunt: After-Hours IAM Activity
widgets.description: Detects IAM configuration changes made outside normal business hours (before 06:00 or after 22:00 UTC). Identity changes at unusual hours may indicate a compromised account being used when legitimate users are not monitoring. Uses time-based anomaly detection (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection - temporal patterns).

widgets.title: Hunt: Defense Evasion Score
widgets.description: Calculates a defense evasion risk score per host by counting distinct evasion indicators: event log clearing, AMSI bypass, firewall modification, UAC bypass, process hollowing. Hosts exhibiting multiple evasion techniques indicate sophisticated attackers actively disabling security controls. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - risk scoring).

dashboards.name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
dashboards.description: Sysmon network connection analysis: lateral movement detection, C2 beacon identification, DNS tunneling, named pipe activity, and MITRE ATT&CK technique mapping.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: C2 Beacon Candidates
widgets.query_title: Sysmon C2 Beacon - Periodic Outbound HTTPS
widgets.description: Detects periodic outbound HTTPS connections to non-standard destinations that may indicate C2 beaconing activity. Suspicious processes making repeated connections to external IPs on port 443 are flagged.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: Lateral Movement - SMB
widgets.description: Detects SMB connections (port 445) from processes commonly used for lateral movement. Attackers use PsExec, WMI, and other tools to move laterally through SMB/Windows Admin Shares.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: Lateral Movement - WinRM
widgets.description: Detects WinRM/PSRemoting connections (ports 5985/5986) from suspicious processes. Attackers use PowerShell Remoting for lateral movement and remote command execution.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: RDP Lateral Movement
widgets.description: Detects outbound RDP connections (port 3389) from unusual processes. While RDP is commonly used for administration, connections from non-standard processes may indicate lateral movement.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: DNS Tunnel Indicators
widgets.query_title: Sysmon DNS Tunneling via Network Connection
widgets.description: Detects suspicious processes making DNS connections (port 53) that could indicate DNS tunneling for data exfiltration or C2 communication.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: LOLBin Outbound Traffic
widgets.query_title: Sysmon Suspicious Outbound Connection from LOLBin
widgets.description: Detects outbound network connections from Living-off-the-Land binaries (LOLBins) to external IP addresses. These binaries are commonly abused by attackers for downloading payloads and establishing C2.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: Kerberoasting Network
widgets.description: Detects Kerberos ticket requests (port 88) from anomalous processes. Kerberoasting attacks request service tickets (TGS) to crack service account passwords offline.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: LDAP Reconnaissance
widgets.description: Detects LDAP enumeration patterns (port 389/636) from suspicious processes. Attackers use LDAP queries to enumerate Active Directory users, groups, computers, and service accounts.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: File: Executable Dropped
widgets.query_title: Sysmon Executable File Created or Detected
widgets.description: Detects Sysmon Event ID 29 FileExecutableDetected and Event ID 11 executable file creates in writable or startup locations.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: Cobalt Strike C2
widgets.query_title: Sysmon Cobalt Strike C2 Network Indicators
widgets.description: Detects network connections from processes commonly associated with Cobalt Strike beacon activity. Includes default Cobalt Strike process injection targets and malleable C2 patterns.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Net: Mimikatz Network
widgets.description: Detects network connections from mimikatz or known credential theft tools accessing domain controller services (LDAP, Kerberos, SMB).

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Pipe: Cobalt Strike Pipes
widgets.description: Detects named pipe creation matching known Cobalt Strike default patterns. Cobalt Strike uses named pipes for inter-process communication and lateral movement via SMB beacon.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Pipe: PsExec Pipes
widgets.description: Detects named pipe creation matching PsExec default pipe names. PsExec creates named pipes for remote command execution and is commonly used for lateral movement.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Pipe: Mimikatz Pipes
widgets.description: Detects named pipe creation matching known Mimikatz pipe patterns. Mimikatz uses named pipes for credential extraction and token manipulation operations.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: Pipe: Suspicious C2 Pipes
widgets.description: Detects named pipe creation matching common C2 framework patterns including Metasploit, Covenant, and other post-exploitation tools.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: DNS: Data Exfiltration
widgets.description: Detects DNS queries with high-entropy subdomains that may indicate data exfiltration via DNS tunneling. Encoded data in subdomain labels is a common technique for covert data transfer.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: DNS: C2 Framework Domains
widgets.query_title: Sysmon DNS Query to Known C2 Framework Domains
widgets.description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with known C2 frameworks and dynamic DNS services commonly used by attackers for C2 infrastructure.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Sysmon Network and Lateral Movement Dashboard
widgets.title: DNS: Suspicious TLDs
widgets.query_title: Sysmon DNS Query to Suspicious TLDs
widgets.description: Detects DNS queries to top-level domains frequently associated with malicious infrastructure, phishing, and C2 operations.

dashboards.name: C2 & Beaconing Detection
dashboards.description: Enhanced command-and-control investigation dashboard for DNS beacons, HTTPS callbacks, suspicious C2 destinations, VCN/Network Firewall egress, and host-to-destination link analysis across the 21-day demo dataset.

widgets.title: Top C2 DNS Domains
widgets.description: Ranks suspicious DNS queries for dynamic DNS, high-risk TLDs, and demo DNS exfiltration domains. This replaces the legacy endsWith-based dashboard query with LAQL patterns that return rows from current Sysmon Operational DNS events.
ask_ai_prompts: Which DNS domains look like C2 or exfiltration infrastructure?
Group the domains by dynamic DNS, suspicious TLD, and DNS exfiltration evidence.

widgets.title: Beacon Activity Timeline
widgets.description: Shows C2 activity over time across Sysmon DNS/network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall telemetry. The timeline helps analysts identify recurring callbacks and pivot into the source and destination widgets.
ask_ai_prompts: Identify the first and last C2 beacon windows.
Which log source provides the strongest recurring beacon evidence?

widgets.title: DNS Beacon Queries
widgets.description: Counts all suspicious DNS beacon and DNS exfiltration lookups from Sysmon Operational DNS events for the 21-day demo window.
ask_ai_prompts: Explain the DNS beacon query count and the domains driving it.
Which hosts should be investigated first based on DNS beacon volume?

widgets.title: C2 Destination IPs
widgets.description: Summarizes suspicious C2 destinations across Sysmon network events, VCN Flow Logs, and Network Firewall logs. Includes hostname and byte evidence when those fields are present.
ask_ai_prompts: Which source and destination pair is the strongest C2 candidate?
Use the byte evidence to separate beaconing from exfiltration.

widgets.title: C2 Communication Topology
widgets.description: Link analysis for source-to-destination C2 communications across Sysmon network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall logs. Use it to pivot from a compromised machine or private IP to the external C2 destination.

visualization_options.tileLayoutXml: <title-text>Peak Path</title-text><title-text>Events</title-text><title-text>Sources</title-text><title-text>Destinations</title-text>

ask_ai_prompts: Trace the C2 path from source IP to destination IP.
Which source IP appears compromised and what evidence supports that conclusion?

widgets.title: DNS Beacon Sources
widgets.description: Identifies hosts and source processes issuing suspicious DNS beacon or DNS exfiltration lookups. This provides the host pivot for the C2 DNS widgets.
ask_ai_prompts: Which host and process issued the most suspicious DNS queries?
List the first host to isolate based on DNS beaconing evidence.

widgets.title: Unique C2 Domains
widgets.description: Counts the distinct suspicious C2 and DNS exfiltration domains queried during the demo window.
ask_ai_prompts: Which unique domains should be added to containment or DNS block lists?
Separate C2 domains from DNS exfiltration domains.

widgets.title: C2 Flow Connections
widgets.description: Counts C2 and exfiltration flow evidence observed by VCN Flow Logs and Network Firewall logs for the known external destination.
ask_ai_prompts: How many network flow records prove C2 or exfiltration?
Which flow log source should be used for containment evidence?

widgets.title: HTTPS Beaconing (Port 443)
widgets.description: Shows repeated HTTPS callbacks to suspicious destinations across Sysmon network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall telemetry. Port 443 rows with high connection counts are the main C2 beaconing evidence.
ask_ai_prompts: Which HTTPS destination shows periodic beaconing behavior?
Identify whether the same destination also has high BytesSent evidence.

widgets.title: Affected Hosts
widgets.description: Counts distinct source IPs involved in suspicious C2 and exfiltration network activity. This gives a reliable affected-system KPI across Sysmon network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall sources.
ask_ai_prompts: Which source IPs should be treated as compromised machines?
Correlate the affected source IPs with DNS and firewall evidence.

dashboards.name: SOC: FreeLabFriday Threat Hunting Dashboard
dashboards.description: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday-inspired hunting dashboard for DNS C2, BITS and cloud-service exfiltration, domain-fronted C2, vsagent HTTP beaconing, credential stuffing, rogue user persistence, and port-knocking drilldowns.

widgets.title: FLF: DNS C2 Patterns
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for DNS command-and-control patterns: long or suspicious DNS query names, dynamic DNS infrastructure, high-risk TLDs, and repeated queries by host.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Domain Name System As C2

ask_ai_prompts: Which host has DNS query patterns most consistent with DNS C2?
List the suspicious query names and explain whether the activity looks like beaconing or exfiltration.

widgets.title: FLF: BITS Exfiltration
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for BITS abuse through bitsadmin.exe or Start-BitsTransfer commands that reference external HTTP/HTTPS destinations or suspicious file-transfer behavior.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - BITS As Exfil

ask_ai_prompts: Which BITS commands or network events look like exfiltration rather than update traffic?
Identify the host, command line, and destination that should be investigated first.

widgets.title: FLF: Cloud Service Exfiltration
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for suspicious non-browser processes uploading or connecting to trusted cloud services such as Microsoft Graph, OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, and Slack.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Cloud-Based Services as Exfil

ask_ai_prompts: Which non-browser process is using trusted cloud services in a suspicious way?
Correlate this cloud-service activity with file discovery, staging, or outbound byte-volume evidence.

widgets.title: FLF: Domain Fronting CDN C2
widgets.query_title: FLF: Domain Fronting CDN C2 Hunt
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for suspicious C2 candidates where non-browser processes repeatedly connect to CDN or cloud-fronting endpoints over HTTPS.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Domain Fronting as C2

ask_ai_prompts: Which endpoint process/CDN pair looks most like domain-fronted C2?
What additional TLS, proxy, or firewall evidence should be checked for SNI and Host-header mismatch?

widgets.title: FLF: vsagent HTTP Beaconing
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday AdvancedC2 hunt for vsagent-style HTTP beacons: fixed User-Agent, repeated GET /beacon check-ins, and POST /beacon output submission.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - vsagent HTTP Beaconing

ask_ai_prompts: Does this HTTP beacon pattern show regular implant check-ins?
Pivot from the User-Agent and /beacon URI to client IP, HTTP method, and any POST output events.

widgets.title: FLF: Credential Stuffing
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for automated credential stuffing across Windows and application authentication logs, highlighting high-volume attempts against many accounts from the same infrastructure.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Credential Stuffing

ask_ai_prompts: Which source infrastructure is attempting logins against the most accounts?
Look for repeated failures followed by success and summarize likely compromised accounts.

widgets.title: FLF: New User Persistence
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for attacker persistence through account creation. Windows Event ID 4720 is surfaced with host, account, and source context for triage.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - New User Added

ask_ai_prompts: Which newly-created account looks suspicious and why?
Pivot to privilege assignment, group membership, and logon events around this account creation.

widgets.title: FLF: Port Knocking Sequence
widgets.description: FreeLabFriday-inspired link analysis for port knocking: knocks on 7000, 8000, and 9000 followed by SSH on 22, with failed wrong-order attempts preserved for comparison.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Port Knocking on the Wire

visualization_options.tileLayoutXml: <title-text>Peak Path</title-text><title-text>Events</title-text><title-text>Sources</title-text><title-text>Targets</title-text>

ask_ai_prompts: Which source completed the 7000 to 8000 to 9000 knock sequence and then reached SSH?
Compare the successful sequence with failed wrong-order attempts and identify the protected server.

dashboards.name: SOC: 2025-2026 Threat Hunting Dashboard
dashboards.description: MELTS-driven threat-hunting dashboard for 2025-2026 attack tradecraft: ClickFix/CrashFix, SharePoint ToolShell, RMM abuse, AiTM token replay, compromised-machine drilldowns, and exfiltration evidence.

widgets.title: MELTS: Signal Overview
widgets.description: Correlates Metrics-style counts, Events, Logs, Traces, and Security state for the modern 2025-2026 demo attacks: ClickFix/CrashFix, SharePoint ToolShell, RMM abuse, AiTM token replay, and staged exfiltration.

references.name: MELT observability pattern: Metrics, Events, Logs, and Traces

ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the 2025-2026 campaign by MELTS signal type and attack stage.
Which trace IDs and log sources provide the strongest evidence?

widgets.title: MELTS: Attack Timeline
widgets.description: Time-series view of the modern attack scenarios across endpoint, web, application, network, firewall, and cloud-control-plane logs. Use this as the first pivot before drilling into attack-specific widgets.
ask_ai_prompts: Build a chronological threat-hunting timeline for these attacks.
Which log source saw the entry point first?

widgets.title: MELTS: Attack Path Link
widgets.description: Link analysis for the 2025-2026 campaign. Groups trace IDs to log sources so analysts can pivot from entry point to host execution, cloud abuse, C2, and exfiltration evidence.
