ask_ai_prompts: Trace each attack path from entry point to exfiltration.
Identify missing telemetry for any trace group.

widgets.title: ClickFix: Clipboard PowerShell
widgets.description: Detects fake CAPTCHA / ClickFix social-engineering execution patterns where a browser-spawned process runs PowerShell with clipboard-themed instructions, download cradles, or hidden execution.
ask_ai_prompts: Explain why this looks like ClickFix rather than normal PowerShell automation.
List the parent browser, command line, host, and trace ID for containment.

widgets.title: ClickFix: LOLBin Payloads
widgets.description: Detects ClickFix follow-on payload execution through mshta, rundll32, or scriptlet-style LOLBins launched from browser context.
ask_ai_prompts: Which signed binaries were abused after the ClickFix entry point?
Prioritize commands that downloaded or executed remote script content.

widgets.title: CrashFix: Python RAT
widgets.description: Detects CrashFix-style ClickFix variants where browser social engineering leads to Python RAT execution, package retrieval, and HTTPS callback activity.

references.name: Microsoft: ClickFix variant CrashFix deploying Python RAT trojan

ask_ai_prompts: Tie the Python process execution to outbound CrashFix callback evidence.
Which host should be isolated first and why?

widgets.title: SharePoint: ToolShell Attempts
widgets.description: Detects SharePoint ToolShell-style exploitation by looking for ToolPane and spinstall0.aspx activity across WAF, load balancer, web application, and application trace telemetry.

references.name: Microsoft MSRC: Customer guidance for SharePoint vulnerability CVE-2025-53770

ask_ai_prompts: Identify the first ToolShell exploitation attempt and the affected SharePoint host.
Which requests reached the backend instead of being blocked at WAF?

widgets.title: SharePoint: Webshell Post-Exploit
widgets.description: Finds post-exploitation command attempts against SharePoint webshell paths, especially spinstall0.aspx command execution, backend 500s, and high-severity application spans.

references.name: Microsoft MSRC: SharePoint CVE-2025-53770 customer guidance

ask_ai_prompts: Show the webshell command attempts and whether WAF blocked them.
Use trace ID trace_toolshell_sp_001 to reconstruct the SharePoint chain.

widgets.title: RMM: Post-Compromise Activity
widgets.description: Detects remote monitoring and management tooling used after compromise, including ScreenConnect, AnyDesk, and Atera process execution plus outbound relay traffic.

references.name: CISA: Remote Monitoring and Management software security guidance

ask_ai_prompts: Which RMM tools were launched and which relay hosts were contacted?
Differentiate expected IT management from post-compromise remote access.

widgets.title: Cloud Identity: AiTM Token Abuse
widgets.description: Detects code-of-conduct themed AiTM/token-replay behavior by following one principal through login, discovery, Object Storage access, and auth-token creation.

references.name: Microsoft: Multi-stage code-of-conduct phishing campaign leads to AiTM token compromise

ask_ai_prompts: Was this one-time login or sustained token abuse across cloud APIs?
List the abused identity, source IP, cloud resources, and containment actions.

widgets.title: Exfil: After Initial Access
widgets.description: Finds large outbound transfers and firewall data-theft alerts tied to ClickFix/CrashFix, web-to-cloud, and cloud identity abuse traces.
ask_ai_prompts: Which data objects or destinations are tied to exfiltration?
Identify the entry point and compromised machines for each exfiltration trace.

widgets.title: Compromised Machines and Data
widgets.description: Sunburst-ready rollup showing which machines, identities, and data movement signals are tied to the modern attack traces.
ask_ai_prompts: Which machines and identities are compromised across the modern attack set?
Use the grouped signals to recommend containment order.

dashboards.name: SOC: Web Application Security Dashboard
dashboards.description: OWASP Top 10 web attack detection via WAF, Load Balancer, and application logs. SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, SSRF, command injection, and more.

widgets.title: WAF: SQL Injection Blocked
widgets.description: Detects SQL injection attempts blocked by OCI WAF. Indicates active exploitation of OWASP A03:2021 Injection vulnerabilities against the web application.

widgets.title: WAF: SQL Injection Allowed
widgets.query_title: WAF SQL Injection Attack Allowed Through
widgets.description: Detects SQL injection attempts that were ALLOWED through WAF (detection-only mode or rule bypass). Critical alert indicating potential successful exploitation.

widgets.title: WAF: XSS Attack Blocked
widgets.description: Detects XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attempts blocked by OCI WAF. Part of OWASP A03:2021 Injection category.

widgets.title: WAF: Path Traversal Blocked
widgets.description: Detects directory traversal (path traversal) attacks blocked by WAF. Attackers attempt to access files outside the web root using ../ sequences. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: WAF: Command Injection Blocked
widgets.description: Detects OS command injection attempts blocked by OCI WAF. Attackers inject system commands through web parameters. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

widgets.title: WAF: SSRF Attack Blocked
widgets.description: Detects SSRF attempts blocked by WAF where attackers try to make the server fetch internal resources. OWASP A10:2021 Server-Side Request Forgery.

widgets.title: WAF: XXE Attack Blocked
widgets.query_title: WAF XML External Entity Attack Blocked
widgets.description: Detects XXE (XML External Entity) injection attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers exploit XML parsers to read files, perform SSRF, or execute denial of service.

widgets.title: WAF: SSTI Attack Blocked
widgets.description: Detects Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers inject template syntax to achieve remote code execution via template engines (Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker).

widgets.title: WAF: Log4Shell Blocked
widgets.description: Detects Log4Shell (Log4j RCE) exploitation attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers use JNDI lookup strings to achieve remote code execution. OWASP A06:2021 Vulnerable and Outdated Components.

widgets.title: WAF: NoSQL Injection Blocked
widgets.description: Detects NoSQL injection attempts (MongoDB, CouchDB) blocked by WAF. Attackers inject NoSQL query operators to bypass authentication or extract data. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

widgets.title: WAF: LDAP Injection Blocked
widgets.description: Detects LDAP injection attempts blocked by WAF. Attackers manipulate LDAP queries to bypass authentication or extract directory information. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

widgets.title: WAF: Web Shell Upload Blocked
widgets.query_title: WAF Web Shell Upload Attempt Blocked
widgets.description: Detects attempts to upload web shell files blocked by WAF. Web shells provide persistent remote access through the web server. OWASP A03:2021 Injection.

widgets.title: WAF: Protocol Attack Blocked
widgets.description: Detects HTTP protocol violation attacks blocked by WAF (request smuggling, header injection, HTTP splitting). OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration.

widgets.title: WAF: Rate Limiting Triggered
widgets.description: Detects WAF rate limiting events indicating automated attack tools or brute force attempts. Rate limiting is configured at 100 requests per minute for Seven Kingdoms Portal.

widgets.title: WAF: CORS Bypass Blocked
widgets.description: Detects CORS misconfiguration exploitation attempts where attackers try to bypass cross-origin restrictions. OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration.

widgets.title: LB: Vulnerability Scanner
widgets.description: Detects known web vulnerability scanner user agents in Load Balancer access logs. Indicates active reconnaissance against the web application.

widgets.title: LB: Brute Force Login
widgets.query_title: Web Application Brute Force Login Attempt
widgets.description: Detects repeated failed login attempts via HTTP 401/403 responses to authentication endpoints. Indicates brute force or credential stuffing attacks. OWASP A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures.

widgets.title: LB: Directory Enumeration
widgets.description: Detects directory and file enumeration attempts via high volume of 404 responses to common admin/config paths. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: LB: Sensitive Data Access
widgets.description: Detects access to endpoints that may expose sensitive data (database exports, configuration files, backup files). OWASP A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures / A01 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: LB: HTTP Method Abuse
widgets.description: Detects use of unusual or dangerous HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE, OPTIONS, PATCH) that may indicate API abuse or security misconfiguration. OWASP A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration.

widgets.title: LB: Large Response Exfil
widgets.description: Detects unusually large HTTP responses that may indicate successful data exfiltration (database dumps, bulk user data extraction). OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: LB: Server Errors
widgets.description: Detects HTTP 500 Internal Server Error responses which may indicate successful injection attacks causing application errors, or application instability from attack traffic.

widgets.title: LB: API Unauthorized
widgets.description: Detects unauthorized access attempts to API endpoints (401/403 responses). May indicate API key brute force, token manipulation, or broken access control. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: LB: Suspicious User Agent
widgets.query_title: Suspicious or Empty User Agent Detected
widgets.description: Detects requests with empty, missing, or known-malicious user agents. Automated attack tools often use non-standard user agents or omit them entirely.

widgets.title: App: IDOR Detected
widgets.description: Detects IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) attacks where users access resources belonging to other users by manipulating object IDs in URLs. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: App: Privilege Escalation
widgets.description: Detects privilege escalation attempts where users try to modify their role or access admin-only endpoints. OWASP A01:2021 Broken Access Control.

widgets.title: App: Auth Bypass
widgets.description: Detects authentication bypass attempts including JWT manipulation, session fixation, and forced browsing to protected endpoints. OWASP A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures.

widgets.title: App: Insecure Deserialization
widgets.description: Detects insecure deserialization attacks where malicious serialized objects are sent to achieve remote code execution. OWASP A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures.

widgets.title: App: Session Hijacking
widgets.description: Detects indicators of session hijacking or session fixation attacks where an attacker steals or manipulates session tokens. OWASP A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures.

widgets.title: App: Mass Assignment
widgets.description: Detects mass assignment attacks where extra parameters are injected into API requests to modify protected fields (isAdmin, role, price). OWASP A04:2021 Insecure Design.

dashboards.name: SOC: Web Threat Hunting Dashboard
dashboards.description: Advanced web application threat hunting analytics. WAF attack frequency, SQL injection stacking, multi-vector scoring, geographic anomalies, and multi-stage attack chain correlation.

widgets.title: Hunt: WAF Attack by Source IP
widgets.query_title: WAF Attack Frequency by Source IP (Frequency Analysis)
widgets.description: Identifies source IPs generating the most WAF block events. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking) to surface prolific attackers targeting the web application. IPs with high block counts indicate automated attack tools.

widgets.title: Hunt: SQLi Pattern Stacking
widgets.query_title: SQL Injection Pattern Stacking (Rare Value Detection)
widgets.description: Stacks unique SQL injection payloads observed in WAF events to identify attack tool signatures and novel injection techniques. Rare payloads may indicate custom exploits or new attack vectors. Uses rare value stacking (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking).

widgets.title: Hunt: Web Brute Force
widgets.query_title: Web Application Brute Force Detection (Frequency Analysis)
widgets.description: Identifies IPs performing brute force attacks against web login endpoints by counting 401/403 responses. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking) to surface credential stuffing and brute force campaigns.

widgets.title: Hunt: Directory Scan Cluster
widgets.query_title: Web Directory Scanning IP Clustering (Anomaly Detection)
widgets.description: Clusters IPs performing directory enumeration by counting distinct 404-generating paths. A high number of unique 404 paths from a single IP indicates systematic directory/file scanning using tools like dirb, gobuster, or ffuf.

widgets.title: Hunt: Multi-Attack Scoring
widgets.description: Calculates a threat score per source IP based on the diversity of attack types (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, command injection, SSRF). An IP triggering multiple different CRS rule categories indicates a sophisticated attacker using multiple techniques. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods).

widgets.title: Hunt: Attack Geo Anomaly
widgets.query_title: Web Attack Geographic Anomaly (Rare Country Detection)
widgets.description: Identifies attack traffic originating from unusual countries by stacking WAF events by country code. Rare countries with high attack volumes may indicate targeted campaigns from specific threat actor regions.

widgets.title: Hunt: OWASP Multi-Stage
widgets.query_title: OWASP Multi-Stage Web Attack Chain (Combined Methods)
widgets.description: Correlates web attack indicators across WAF, Load Balancer, and application logs to identify multi-stage attack chains. An IP that triggers WAF blocks, generates auth failures, and accesses sensitive endpoints is likely executing a coordinated attack campaign. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).

widgets.title: Hunt: Scanner Tool Stacking
widgets.query_title: Web Scanner Tool Identification (User Agent Stacking)
widgets.description: Stacks user agents across all web traffic to identify automated scanning tools, attack frameworks, and bot traffic. Non-browser user agents generating high request volumes from a small set of source IPs indicate active reconnaissance or automated exploitation.

dashboards.name: SOC: Web-to-Cloud Threat Hunting Dashboard
dashboards.description: Incident-focused dashboard for the web-to-cloud attack chain: SSRF entry point, compromised machines and identity, VCN egress, Network Firewall C2, OCI Audit cloud abuse, and exfiltration evidence with link and sunburst drilldowns.

widgets.title: W2C: Correlated Timeline
widgets.description: Summarizes the web-to-cloud attack chain across WAF, load balancer, application, host, VCN Flow, Network Firewall, Sysmon network, and OCI Audit logs. The shared Trace ID and fixed attacker/C2 pivots let analysts follow entry point, credential access, C2, cloud abuse, and exfiltration in one widget.
ask_ai_prompts: Build a chronological timeline for trace_w2c_entry_001.
Which log sources confirm entry, compromise, and exfiltration?

widgets.title: W2C: Entry Point and SSRF
widgets.query_title: Web-to-Cloud: Entry Point and SSRF Evidence
widgets.description: Identifies the initial web request that reached the application tier and attempted access to the cloud instance metadata endpoint. Use the Request URL, Client IP, backend host, and Trace ID to start the investigation.
ask_ai_prompts: Explain how the SSRF entry point was detected.
Which request URL and source IP started the chain?

widgets.title: W2C: Compromised Machines
widgets.description: Shows hosts and private IPs tied to the compromise, including application host activity, Windows C2 telemetry, VCN flows, and firewall sessions. Analysts can pivot from host names to network tuples and command lines.
ask_ai_prompts: Which machines are implicated in the web-to-cloud chain?
List the private IPs and host names that should be isolated.

widgets.title: W2C: Compromised Identity
widgets.description: Highlights OCI Audit activity performed by the compromised service identity after instance metadata access. The widget surfaces Object Storage enumeration, object reads, and pre-authenticated request creation.
ask_ai_prompts: Which OCI identity was abused after the web entry point?
List the Object Storage actions and resources accessed by the compromised identity.

widgets.title: W2C: VCN Egress
widgets.query_title: Web-to-Cloud: VCN Egress and Exfil Flows
widgets.description: Surfaces accepted VCN Flow Log records from the compromised application host to the C2/exfil destination and OCI service endpoints. Large Bytes Sent values provide the primary network indicator for data loss.
ask_ai_prompts: Which VCN flows show the largest outbound transfer?
Map the compromised private IP to the external C2 destination.

widgets.title: W2C: Network Firewall C2
widgets.query_title: Web-to-Cloud: Network Firewall C2 and Threat Alerts
widgets.description: Correlates Network Firewall traffic and threat records for the C2 destination. Threat Name, Firewall Rule, byte counts, and action values show whether the exfiltration was alerted, allowed, or denied.
ask_ai_prompts: Which Network Firewall rule observed the C2/exfiltration traffic?
Did the firewall allow, alert, or deny the suspicious session?

widgets.title: W2C: OCI Audit Cloud Abuse
widgets.description: Filters OCI Audit for cloud discovery and Object Storage actions associated with the compromised service identity and web-to-cloud Trace ID.
ask_ai_prompts: Which OCI Audit actions prove cloud control-plane abuse?
Identify the bucket and object names touched by the compromised identity.

widgets.title: W2C: Exfiltrated Data
widgets.description: Combines application export messages, VCN Flow egress, Network Firewall threat alerts, and OCI Audit object reads to show what data left the environment and which destination received it.
ask_ai_prompts: What data was exfiltrated and which logs prove it?
Connect the Object Storage object read to the outbound network destination.

widgets.title: W2C: Attack Path Link
widgets.description: Link analysis groups all events carrying the web-to-cloud Trace ID or C2/object-storage pivots into a single path. Use this widget to drill from WAF entry to app host, network egress, firewall alert, and OCI Audit object access.

visualization_options.tileLayoutXml: <title-text>Peak Path</title-text><title-text>Evidence</title-text><title-text>Traces</title-text><title-text>Log Sources</title-text>

ask_ai_prompts: Trace the attack path from entry point through exfiltration.
Which stage has the strongest evidence and which needs more investigation?

widgets.title: W2C: MITRE Stage Breakdown
widgets.description: Sunburst-friendly rollup of the web-to-cloud chain by evidence source and threat label. It shows whether evidence is present across initial access, host, network, firewall, and cloud-control-plane telemetry.
ask_ai_prompts: Which MITRE-style stages have complete evidence coverage?
Which log source contributes most to each attack stage?

dashboards.name: OCI-DEMO: Application 360 Monitoring Dashboard
dashboards.description: 360-degree observability for OCI-DEMO applications (CRM Portal + Drone Shop). Runs on SOC Application Logs, a custom JSON telemetry source that models application, browser, and trace context with a shared Trace ID for correlation.

widgets.title: App: Error Rate by Service
widgets.description: Monitors ERROR-level application logs across CRM Portal and Drone Shop, grouped by service and endpoint. High error rates indicate application health issues requiring investigation.
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize which services and endpoints are driving the current error volume.
Identify whether one error type is dominating this service-level error distribution.

widgets.title: App: Slow Requests (>2s)
widgets.description: Detects requests exceeding 2-second latency across CRM and Drone Shop. Correlates with trace_id for APM drilldown and db.target for database performance investigation.

widgets.title: App: Request Rate by Endpoint
widgets.query_title: Request Rate by Service and Endpoint
widgets.description: HTTP request throughput across CRM and Drone Shop grouped by route and status code. Identifies hotspots, traffic patterns, and potential DDoS indicators.
ask_ai_prompts: Highlight the busiest endpoints and call out any response-code patterns that look abnormal.
Explain whether traffic concentration suggests a hotspot, crawler activity, or brute-force behavior.

widgets.title: App: Service Health Timeline
widgets.description: Tracks application lifecycle and error activity across CRM and Drone Shop in 15-minute service-level buckets. Use for availability monitoring and deployment impact analysis without depending on unsupported raw-message aggregations.
ask_ai_prompts: Explain the major lifecycle or error spikes in this service timeline.
Call out whether the pattern looks like a deployment issue, outage, or normal operational churn.

widgets.title: App: OWASP Attack Detection
widgets.description: Detects security attacks across both applications with MITRE ATT&CK and OWASP Top 10 classification. Correlates attack type, severity, source IP, and trace_id for full incident reconstruction.

widgets.title: App: WAF Signal Correlation
widgets.query_title: WAF Signal Correlation with Application Traces
widgets.description: Correlates OCI WAF signals (x-oci-waf-score, x-oci-waf-action) captured by application middleware with the full request trace. Links WAF detection to application-level security spans and database queries.
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize which client IPs and routes have the strongest WAF signal concentration.
Explain whether the observed WAF scores look like probing, bypass attempts, or noisy false positives.

widgets.title: App: SQLi + XSS Detection
widgets.query_title: SQL Injection and XSS Attack Detection
widgets.description: Detects SQL injection and cross-site scripting attacks against CRM and Drone Shop applications. Captures attack payload, source IP, target endpoint, and APM trace for full forensic analysis.

widgets.title: App: Auth Brute Force
widgets.description: Detects brute force login attempts against CRM Portal and Drone Shop by monitoring failed authentication events. Correlates with WAF signals and rate limiting events.

widgets.title: App: Security Attack by IP
widgets.description: Top attacking IPs across both applications with attack type distribution and severity breakdown. Enables IP-based threat intelligence correlation and firewall rule recommendations.

widgets.title: App: Cross-Service Trace Correlation
widgets.description: Correlates distributed traces across CRM Portal and Drone Shop via shared trace_id. Identifies cross-service request flows, order sync operations, and integration health issues. This is the core 360 monitoring query.

widgets.title: App: Order Sync Pipeline
widgets.query_title: Order Sync Pipeline Health (Drone Shop → CRM)
widgets.description: Monitors the cross-service order synchronization pipeline between Drone Shop and CRM Portal. Tracks sync success/failure rates, created/updated orders, and detects sync anomalies.

widgets.title: App: DB Performance Correlation
widgets.description: Correlates application logs with Oracle ATP database context. Uses trace_id (set as CLIENT_IDENTIFIER in V$SESSION) to link Log Analytics entries with OPSI SQL performance and DB Management AWR reports.

dashboards.name: OCI-DEMO: Octo APM Demo Dashboard
dashboards.description: Dedicated APM correlation dashboard for octo-apm-demo data in SOC Application Logs. Correlates request logs, APM span hierarchy, DB spans, Java sidecar errors, API Gateway edge decisions, OSQuery host evidence, payment threats, and metric samples by Trace ID, Span ID, Parent Span ID, Request ID, Run ID, and Attack ID.

widgets.title: Octo APM: RED Metrics
widgets.description: Request, error, and duration-oriented telemetry for the Octo APM demo application. Groups metric samples by service, route, and response code so operators can find request volume, error concentration, and slow-path symptoms.
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the Octo APM RED metric signals and identify the hottest route.
Which metric names show elevated errors or latency symptoms?

widgets.title: Octo APM: Request Timeline
widgets.query_title: APM: Octo Demo Request and Error Timeline
widgets.description: Timeline of octo-apm-demo application events by severity, suitable for spotting bursts of warnings, errors, and normal request flow.
ask_ai_prompts: Explain the octo-apm-demo request and error timeline.
Identify whether error events align with request bursts.

widgets.title: Octo APM: Span Hotspots
widgets.query_title: APM: Octo Demo Span Latency Hotspots
widgets.description: Span-level rollup for octo-apm-demo. Shows which span names, DB targets, slow request flags, and error types concentrate the most events.
ask_ai_prompts: Rank the octo-apm-demo span hotspots by trace impact.
Which spans look slow, failed, or database-bound?

widgets.title: Octo APM: Trace Logs Correlation
widgets.query_title: APM: Octo Demo Trace to Log Correlation
widgets.description: Correlates octo-apm-demo logs and span context by Trace ID. Use this table to pivot from a trace to its spans, routes, response codes, and error types.
ask_ai_prompts: For the busiest octo-apm-demo trace, explain the related log messages.
Which traces contain both errors and multiple spans?

widgets.title: Octo APM: Trace Investigation Tiles
widgets.query_title: APM: Octo Demo Trace Investigation Tiles
widgets.description: A link-and-tiles investigation view for Octo APM traces. It groups logs, spans, attack evidence, API Gateway edge signals, Java errors, DB spans, and payment redirect indicators by Trace ID so analysts can move from APM Trace Explorer into Log Analytics without losing context.

widgets.title: APT37: Drive-by Compromise
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Internet Explorer Drive-by Compromise
widgets.description: Detects Internet Explorer making network connections to non-Microsoft domains. BLUELIGHT (S0657) uses browser exploits (CVE-2020-1380, CVE-2021-26411) for initial compromise via watering hole attacks. APT37/InkySquid abuses IE vulnerabilities to deliver the BLUELIGHT payload through malicious JavaScript on compromised sites.

widgets.title: APT37: Browser Child Process
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Browser Spawning Suspicious Child Process
widgets.description: Detects Internet Explorer spawning command interpreters or script hosts. After exploiting CVE-2020-1380 or CVE-2021-26411, BLUELIGHT uses iexplore.exe to spawn cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or wscript.exe for second-stage payload execution.

widgets.title: APT37: Obfuscated Commandline
widgets.description: Detects obfuscation techniques in command lines indicative of BLUELIGHT payload delivery. The RAT uses XOR encryption (key 0xCF), Base64 encoding, and SVG-embedded JavaScript for defense evasion during initial exploitation.

widgets.title: APT37: Graph API C2
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: C2 via Microsoft Graph API
widgets.description: Detects non-standard processes connecting to Microsoft Graph API endpoints. BLUELIGHT uses Microsoft Graph API and OneDrive for command-and-control, blending with legitimate Microsoft traffic to evade network detection.

widgets.title: APT37: WMI System Discovery
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: WMI System Enumeration from Browser Child
widgets.description: Detects WMI-based system enumeration spawned by browser processes. After initial exploitation, BLUELIGHT queries Win32_ComputerSystem, Win32_OperatingSystem, and Win32_Processor to profile the target system before establishing full C2.

widgets.title: APT37: Registry Enumeration
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Registry Enumeration of Security Products
widgets.description: Detects registry access targeting security product and installed software keys. BLUELIGHT enumerates SecurityCenter2, Uninstall keys, and AV service registrations to identify defensive tools before attempting further payload delivery.

widgets.title: APT37: File Discovery
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: File Discovery from Browser Process
widgets.description: Detects recursive directory enumeration spawned by browser or script host processes. BLUELIGHT performs file and directory discovery to locate sensitive documents, credentials, and system files for later collection and exfiltration.

widgets.title: APT37: Screen Capture
widgets.description: Detects rapid JPEG file creation by non-image processes. BLUELIGHT captures screenshots at regular intervals and saves them as .jpg files before staging for exfiltration via OneDrive. High frequency (>3 per minute) indicates automated capture.

widgets.title: APT37: Browser Credential Theft
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Browser Credential Memory Access
widgets.description: Detects non-browser processes accessing browser process memory with full access rights. BLUELIGHT reads browser memory (GrantedAccess 0x1fffff = PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS) to extract stored credentials, session tokens, and cookies from Chrome, Edge, Firefox.

widgets.title: APT37: Ingress Tool Transfer
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Executable Download via Graph API
widgets.description: Detects executable or DLL files dropped in temporary directories by processes that also connect to Microsoft Graph API. BLUELIGHT downloads additional tools from OneDrive via Graph API and stages them in Temp or AppData for execution.

widgets.title: APT37: OneDrive Exfiltration
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: Data Exfiltration via OneDrive/Graph API
widgets.description: Detects large data uploads to Microsoft Graph API from non-OneDrive processes. BLUELIGHT exfiltrates collected screenshots, credentials, and system data by uploading to OneDrive via Graph API, blending with legitimate cloud storage traffic.

widgets.title: YARA: PDB Path Indicators
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA PDB Path Indicators (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight)
widgets.description: Detects BLUELIGHT malware PDB path strings in process memory, file content, or Sysmon ImageLoaded events. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight (id: 3ec2d44c). The PDB paths 'Development\BACKDOOR\ncov\' and 'Release\bluelight.pdb' are unique to BLUELIGHT compilation artifacts.

widgets.title: YARA: System Recon JSON
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA System Reconnaissance JSON (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight)
widgets.description: Detects BLUELIGHT system reconnaissance data collection pattern. The malware collects UserName, ComName, OS, OnlineIP, LocalIP, Time, Compiled, Process Level, AntiVirus, and VM detection results in a specific JSON format and exfiltrates via OneDrive. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight $msg strings.

widgets.title: YARA: Cookie Theft (Chrome/Edge)
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Chrome/Edge Cookie Theft (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
widgets.description: Detects BLUELIGHT browser cookie theft targeting Google authentication. The malware extracts SID, OSID, __Secure-3PSID cookies from Chrome/Edge to hijack Gmail and Google Account sessions. Enables IMAP access on victim Gmail accounts for persistent email exfiltration. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $chrome strings.

widgets.title: YARA: Keylogger Staging
widgets.description: Detects BLUELIGHT keylogger component strings. The malware logs keystrokes with modifier prefixes (SHIFT+, CTRL+, CAPSLOCK+, ALT+) and special key markers ([TAB], [RETURN], [ESC], [ARROW *], [DEL], [WIN]). The keylogger output is staged for exfiltration via OneDrive. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $keylogger_component strings (27 of 29 required).

widgets.title: YARA: Google App C2
widgets.query_title: BLUELIGHT RAT: YARA Google App C2 Communication (APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B)
widgets.description: Detects BLUELIGHT C2 communication via Google services. The variant uses custom Google App authentication with spoofed Firefox User-Agent and Korean language Accept-Language headers (ko-KR). Accesses Gmail and Google Account settings to enable IMAP for email exfiltration. Based on Volexity YARA rule APT_MAL_Win_BlueLight_B $chrome strings.

widgets.title: Hunt: BLUELIGHT Kill Chain
widgets.description: Hunting query that correlates multiple BLUELIGHT (S0657) kill chain stages on the same host. Detects hosts exhibiting 3+ stages of the BLUELIGHT attack chain within 24 hours: browser exploitation, C2 setup, discovery, credential theft, and exfiltration.

dashboards.name: SOC: Browser Attack Detection Dashboard
dashboards.description: Browser-side attack detection using SOC Application Logs, a custom OpenTelemetry-shaped JSON source for browser and application telemetry. Showcase row at the top renders cross-tier trace correlation with the WAF and OWASP attack-mix breakdowns. Detection widgets below cover XSS, SQLi, CSRF, session hijacking, clickjacking, DOM attacks, cryptomining, and fingerprinting across monitored applications.

widgets.description: KPI tile counting every SOC Application Logs record carrying an Attack Type tag in the last 24 hours. Headline metric for executive dashboards.

widgets.title: APM: OWASP Attack Mix by Service
widgets.query_title: APM: OWASP Attack Volume by Service
widgets.description: Sunburst nesting Service Name inside Attack Type. Operators can compare which application surfaces face which OWASP categories most frequently and drill outward from a tactic slice to the affected service.
ask_ai_prompts: Which OWASP category dominates browser attacks right now?
Compare attack mix between enterprise-crm-portal and octo-drone-shop.

widgets.title: APM: Browser Attack -> WAF Correlation
widgets.query_title: APM: Browser Attack to WAF Block Correlation
widgets.description: Summary table joining browser-side attack telemetry from SOC Application Logs with the WAF signal that flagged the same Trace ID. Hosts a count of how many traces fired both an APM detection and a WAF block, so the SOC can prove cross-tier defence-in-depth coverage.
ask_ai_prompts: List traces where both APM and WAF observed the same attack.
Which client IP triggered the most cross-tier detections?

widgets.title: APM: Trace Correlation (APM x WAF)
widgets.description: Link analysis grouping events by Trace ID across all SOC sources. Each link group shows the full lifecycle of one logical request: browser telemetry, backend service spans, and any WAF signal that matched the same Trace ID. Use this widget to pivot from a flagged browser attack to the upstream WAF rule that observed the same exchange.

ask_ai_prompts: Which Trace IDs spanned both APM and WAF in the last hour?
For the top-volume trace, list every step in chronological order.

widgets.title: Browser: XSS Attack Detection
widgets.query_title: APM: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Attack in Request
widgets.description: Detects XSS attack payloads in HTTP URLs, query parameters, and request bodies captured by the SOC Application Logs parser from OpenTelemetry-shaped browser/application telemetry. Covers reflected, stored, and DOM-based XSS vectors including script injection, event handlers, and javascript: URIs.

widgets.title: Browser: SQL Injection Detection
widgets.query_title: APM: SQL Injection Attack in Request
widgets.description: Detects SQL injection attack payloads in HTTP URLs and query parameters captured by the SOC Application Logs parser from OpenTelemetry-shaped browser/application telemetry. Covers UNION-based, boolean-based, time-based blind, and error-based injection techniques.

widgets.title: Browser: CSRF Violation
widgets.query_title: APM: CSRF Token Missing or Invalid on State-Changing Request
widgets.description: Detects HTTP POST/PUT/DELETE requests to state-changing endpoints that lack CSRF tokens or have invalid tokens, indicating potential Cross-Site Request Forgery. Uses APM span attributes to detect missing security headers.

widgets.title: Browser: Session Hijacking
widgets.query_title: APM: Session Hijacking - Rapid Session Changes
widgets.description: Detects rapid session ID changes within a single trace or from a single IP, indicating potential session hijacking, session fixation, or cookie theft. Multiple distinct session IDs per source within a short window is anomalous.

widgets.title: Browser: Clickjacking Detection
widgets.query_title: APM: Clickjacking - Missing Frame Protection Headers
widgets.description: Detects HTTP responses missing X-Frame-Options or Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors directives, indicating vulnerability to clickjacking attacks. Monitors APM response headers for missing security controls.

widgets.title: Browser: DOM-Based Attacks
widgets.query_title: APM: DOM-Based Attack via Dangerous JavaScript APIs
widgets.description: Detects DOM-based attacks using dangerous JavaScript APIs observed in browser telemetry spans. Monitors for document.cookie access, eval() calls, innerHTML manipulation, and location/hash-based injection in OpenTelemetry browser spans.

widgets.title: Browser: Suspicious JS Patterns
widgets.description: Detects suspicious JavaScript execution patterns in browser telemetry indicating crypto mining, keylogging, formjacking, or other malicious client-side scripts. Uses OpenTelemetry browser instrumentation to monitor JS execution context.

widgets.title: Browser: Fingerprinting Detection
widgets.description: Detects browser fingerprinting techniques using Canvas API, WebGL parameters, AudioContext, and other APIs commonly used to uniquely identify browsers. Excessive fingerprinting API calls indicate tracking scripts or reconnaissance.

widgets.title: Hunt: Browser Attack Frequency
widgets.query_title: Browser Attack Frequency Analysis (SOC Application Logs)
widgets.description: Hunting query that performs frequency analysis on browser-based attacks detected via SOC Application Logs. Identifies source IPs exhibiting multiple attack types against monitored applications, prioritizing multi-vector attackers.

dashboards.name: SOC: oci-coordinator Hunt Showcase Dashboard
dashboards.description: End-to-end threat-hunting showcase for the oci-coordinator demo. Top row: KPI tiles for total hits, affected hosts, critical alerts, and distinct MITRE techniques across 15 critical-severity scenarios (Linux, OCI Audit, APM, Web). Middle: MITRE tactic→technique sunburst, per-scenario breakdown bar, attack timeline line, and top-affected-hosts horizontal bar. Bottom: drill-down tiles for each of the 15 scenarios — bind shell, boopkit eBPF, container escape, kernel module loaded from temp, passwd/shadow direct mod, exec from /dev/shm, reverse shell, web shell file, SSRF to IMDS, insmod from temp, shadow file read, web process spawning shell, APM SQL injection, insecure deserialization, OCI audit retention reduced.

widgets.title: Total Hunt Hits (l7d)
widgets.description: Total number of events matching any of the 15 oci-coordinator hunting scenarios over the last 7 days. Combines Linux audit/syslog, OCI Audit, APM application, and web/WAF sources.

widgets.title: Affected Hosts (l7d)
widgets.description: Distinct host count across all 15 oci-coordinator hunt scenarios. Surfaces blast radius — how many unique hosts triggered at least one critical-severity scenario over the last 7 days.

widgets.title: Critical Alerts (l7d)
widgets.description: Tile count of CAT I / critical hunt scenarios observed. All 15 scenarios in the oci-coordinator showcase carry critical severity, so this equals the total scenario hits.

widgets.title: MITRE Techniques (l7d)
widgets.description: Distinct count of process names observed across the 15 oci-coordinator hunting scenarios. Higher cardinality signals broader attacker toolchain breadth across the kill-chain.

widgets.title: MITRE Tactic → Technique
widgets.description: Sunburst rollup of scenario hits grouped by Log Source then Process Name. Compact attacker-toolchain view without eval/case (parser-native fields only) — useful for spotting which process families drive each log-source's noise.

widgets.title: Per-Scenario Breakdown
widgets.description: Bar chart of scenario hits grouped by Log Source. Identifies which telemetry stream (Linux audit, APM, Web, OCI Audit) is generating the most threat-hunt noise so analysts pivot to the right log first.

widgets.title: Attack Timeline (1h bins)
widgets.description: Histogram of scenario hits per hour across the last 7 days. Spot bursts of activity and pivot timing between scenarios.

widgets.title: Top Affected Hosts
widgets.description: Horizontal bar chart of hosts ranked by number of scenario hits across the 15 oci-coordinator hunts. Spot stations under sustained attack or pivot points.

widgets.title: Hunt: Bind Shell Listener
widgets.description: Detects creation of bind shell listeners using netcat, socat, or ncat. Bind shells open a listening port that allows remote attackers to connect and execute commands.

widgets.title: Hunt: Boopkit eBPF Rootkit
widgets.description: Detects boopkit/eBPF rootkit activity including BPF program/map loading, XDP attachment, and magic-packet triggered command execution. Boopkit hides reverse shells via eBPF and is invoked by attacker-crafted SYN packets.

widgets.title: Hunt: Container Escape
widgets.description: Detects patterns indicating container escape attempts including access to Docker socket, cgroup manipulation, or nsenter usage from within containers.

widgets.title: Hunt: Kernel Module from Temp
widgets.query_title: Linux Kernel Module Loaded from Temp Directory
widgets.description: Detects loading of kernel modules from temporary directories (/tmp, /dev/shm, /var/tmp), a strong indicator of rootkit installation.

widgets.title: Hunt: Passwd/Shadow Direct Mod
widgets.description: Detects direct writes to /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow files, bypassing normal user management tools. This could indicate account creation for persistence.

widgets.title: Hunt: Exec from /dev/shm
widgets.description: Detects execution of processes from /dev/shm (shared memory filesystem). This in-memory filesystem is frequently used by attackers to stage and execute payloads without touching disk.

widgets.title: Hunt: Reverse Shell
widgets.description: Detects common reverse shell patterns in process execution, indicating potential remote access backdoor activity.

widgets.title: Hunt: Web Shell File Creation
widgets.description: Detects file creation or modification in common web directories that may indicate web shell deployment for persistent remote access.

widgets.title: Hunt: SSRF to Cloud IMDS
widgets.query_title: SSRF to Cloud Instance Metadata Service (Linux)
widgets.description:
Detects outbound HTTP requests to the cloud instance metadata endpoint
(169.254.169.254) from web application processes on Linux. This indicates
Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) exploitation targeting OCI/AWS/GCP/Azure
IMDS to extract instance credentials or configuration.
Covers MITRE ATT&CK T1552.005 (Cloud Instance Metadata API).

widgets.title: Hunt: insmod from Temp
widgets.description: Detects execution of insmod loading modules from non-standard paths.

widgets.title: Hunt: Shadow File Read
widgets.description: Detects execution of shadow with direct access to shadow password file.

widgets.title: Hunt: Web Process Spawning Shell
widgets.query_title: Web Server Process Spawning Shell with Injection Characters (Linux)
widgets.description:
Detects a web application runtime (Python, Node, Java, PHP) spawning a shell
process (bash, sh, dash) with command-line arguments containing injection
characters (semicolons, pipes, backticks). This indicates successful Remote
Code Execution (RCE) via a command injection vulnerability in the web app.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Microsoft Sentinel Endpoint Converted Detections
widgets.title: Sentinel: Office Apps Launching Wscipt
widgets.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Trickbot: Pervasive & underestimated.
Trickbot is a very prevalent piece of malware with an array of malicious capabilities. Originally designed to steal banking credentials, it has since evolved into a modular trojan that can deploy other malware, disable security software, and perform command-and-control (C2) operations.
Trickbot is frequently spread through email. An attacker will send a target a message with an attachment containing a malicious macro. If the target enables the macro, it will write a JScript Encoded (JSE) file to disk (JScript is a Microsoft dialect of ECMAScript). The JSE file will then be launched using wscript.exe to perform a variety of malicious tasks, particularly reconnaissance.
The following query detects when Office applications have launched wscript.exe to run a JSE file.
See Detect rundll.exe being used for reconnaissance and command-and-control for another query related to Trickbot activity.
Reference - https://attack.mitre.org/software/S0266/
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the Microsoft Sentinel converted detection results and identify the highest-risk pivots.

widgets.title: Sentinel: cve-2019-0808-c2
widgets.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Windows 7 zero-day for CVE-2019-0808
CVE-2019-0808 is a vulnerability that allows an attacker to escape the Windows security sandbox and run arbitrary code with admin privileges. This vulnerability affects Windows 7, Windows Server 2008, and Windows Server 2008 R2.
Exploits for CVE-2019-0808 were first observed as part of highly selective attacks using the Nufsys backdoor. Although the Nufsys-associated exploit was first described as a zero-day, the issue has since been patched.
The following query detects possible CVE-2019-0808 exploitation by reporting network communication associated with the Nufsys attacks.
References:
https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2019-0808
https://portal.msrc.microsoft.com/security-guidance/advisory/CVE-2019-0808
ask_ai_prompts: Summarize the Microsoft Sentinel converted detection results and identify the highest-risk pivots.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Microsoft Sentinel Endpoint Converted Detections
widgets.title: Sentinel: locate-dll-loaded-in-memory[Nobelium]
widgets.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Solorigate supply chain attack. Please note that these attacks are currently known as the Nobelium campaign.
Microsoft detects the 2020 SolarWinds supply chain attack implant and its other components as part of a campaign by the Nobelium activity group. Nobelium is the threat actor behind the attack against SolarWinds, which was previously referred to as Solorigate.
Nobelium silently added malicious code to legitimate software updates for Orion, which is IT monitoring software provided by SolarWinds. In this way, malicious dynamic link libraries (DLLs) were distributed to SolarWinds customers.
The following query locates malicious Nobelium-associated DLLs that have been loaded into memory on affected systems.
More Nobelium-related queries can be found listed under the See also section of this document.
References:
https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2020/12/13/customer-guidance-on-recent-nation-state-cyber-attacks/
https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2020/12/18/analyzing-solorigate-the-compromised-dll-file-that-started-a-sophisticated-cyberattack-and-how-microsoft-defender-helps-protect/

widgets.title: Sentinel: DopplePaymer Procdump
widgets.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, Doppelpaymer: More human-operated ransomware. There is also a related blog.
DoppelPaymer is ransomware that is spread manually by human operators. These operators have exhibited extensive knowledge of system administration and common network security misconfigurations. For example, they use SysInternal utilities such as ProcDump to dump credentials from LSASS. They often use these stolen credentials to turn off security software, run malicious commands, and spread malware throughout an organization.
The following query detects ProcDump being used to dump credentials from LSASS.
The See also section below lists links to other queries associated with DoppelPaymer.

widgets.dashboard_name: SOC: Microsoft Sentinel Endpoint Converted Detections
widgets.title: Sentinel: LSASS Credential Dumping with Procdump
widgets.description:
This query was originally published in the threat analytics report, "Exchange Server zero-days exploited in the wild".
In early March 2021, Microsoft released patches for four different zero-day vulnerabilities affecting Microsoft Exchange Server. The vulnerabilities were being used in a coordinated attack. For more information on the vulnerabilities, visit the following links:
1. CVE-2021-26855
2. CVE-2021-26857
3. CVE-2021-26858
4. CVE-2021-27065
The following query looks for evidence of Procdump being used to dump credentials from LSASS, the Local Security Authentication Server. This might indicate an attacker has compromised user accounts.
More queries related to this threat can be found under the See also section of this page.
Reference - https://msrc-blog.microsoft.com/2021/03/02/multiple-security-updates-released-for-exchange-server/
